applied_optimizations.name: Triton kernel implementation for Vanilla RNN
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented Triton kernels for RNN i2h (tanh) and h2o operations. Each program computes one (batch, column) pair. BLOCK_SIZE=64 (wavefront size). Manual tanh using tl.math.exp. Grid: (batch_size, hidden_size) for i2h, (batch_size, output_size) for h2o.

attempted_but_failed.name: Multiple columns per program
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton doesn't support break statements, and conditional writes caused correctness issues

notes: Baseline achieved: 9.33ms vs 13.2ms reference (1.41x speedup, SCORE: 64.1)
Main bottleneck: rnn_i2h_kernel takes 80% of GPU time
Kernel launch overhead is significant: 524,288 launches total (batch=8 * hidden=256 * seq=256)
Optimization opportunity: reduce kernel launches by having each program do more work

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Agent has timed out with no output for 4 consecutive trials (2-5), always trying to write a complex tiled GEMM in Triton. The correct approach is dramatically simpler: use torch.mm/addmm for GEMMs (ro

global_notes: ROCm Triton constraints: tl.math.tanh unavailable, use manual implementation with tl.math.exp
Triton doesn't support break statements in kernels
